text.title: Ollama Setup and Model Management — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-setup-models methodology. Sources: Ollama documentation — install / pull / list; Ollama model library (registry.ollama.ai); GGUF quantisation format spec.

rule.statement: Pick a quantisation tier whose memory footprint fits in 80% of available VRAM (or RAM if CPU-only); use the published model→memory table.
rule.rationale: Models that just barely fit in VRAM thrash; the 80% rule leaves headroom for KV-cache growth on long contexts.

rule.statement: Pull the `:instruct` (or `:chat`) tag, not the base model; never run agent workflows against a base completion model.
rule.rationale: Base models complete next-token; instruct models follow instructions. Mixing them up explains many 'model is broken' reports.

rule.statement: Verify install with `curl http://localhost:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"<m>","prompt":"hello"}'`; only proceed when the smoke returns a coherent response.
rule.rationale: Skipping the smoke step lets you debug an agent issue that is actually a model-pull failure.

rule.statement: Pin pulled model tags in a `models.lock` file (model:tag@digest) checked into the repo; never rely on `latest`.

rationale.source: Apple MLX vs llama.cpp benchmark notes

rule.rationale: `latest` rolls under you; a 6-month-old prompt against a refreshed `latest` is a different model.

rule.statement: Allocate ≥ 100 GB free disk for the model cache; clean up unused tags via `ollama rm` weekly.
rule.rationale: Model files run 4–80 GB each; an unmanaged cache fills the disk in weeks and silently breaks new pulls.
